# ApiGear

> ApiGear generates native SDKs from stateful API definitions. Define interfaces once using ObjectAPI, then generate production-ready code for C++, Qt, Unreal Engine, or Python.

ApiGear is an open-source tool by Epic Games that takes API definitions (in YAML or IDL format) and generates complete, buildable SDKs with interfaces, stubs, tests, build files, and monitoring adapters.

## Key Concepts

- ObjectAPI: A specification for stateful APIs with properties (observable state), operations (methods), and signals (events)
- Solution File: Configuration binding API modules to templates for code generation
- Templates: Code generators for specific platforms (C++14, C++17, Qt6, Unreal Engine, Python)
- Simulation: JavaScript-scripted mock backends sharing the same API surface
- Monitoring: Real-time inspection of property changes, operation calls, and signals

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](https://apigear.io/docs/guide/quick-start): Install and generate your first SDK
- [ObjectAPI Specification](https://apigear.io/docs/objectapi/intro): The stateful API specification
- [ObjectAPI IDL](https://apigear.io/docs/objectapi/idl): Developer-friendly IDL syntax
- [ObjectAPI Modules](https://apigear.io/docs/objectapi/modules): Interfaces, structs, enums
- [CLI Reference](https://apigear.io/docs/cli/intro): Command-line tool documentation
- [Code Generation](https://apigear.io/docs/cli/generate): Solution and expert mode generation
- [API Simulation](https://apigear.io/docs/cli/simulate): Script and scenario-based testing
- [MCP Server](https://apigear.io/docs/cli/mcp): AI assistant integration via Model Context Protocol
- [Template Maker](https://apigear.io/docs/sdk/maker/intro): Create custom code generation templates

## Template Documentation

- [C++17 Template](https://apigear.io/template-cpp17/docs/intro)
- [C++14 Template](https://apigear.io/template-cpp14/docs/intro)
- [Unreal Engine Template](https://apigear.io/template-unreal/docs/intro)
- [Qt6 Template](https://apigear.io/template-qt6/docs/intro)
- [Python Template](https://apigear.io/template-python/docs/intro)

## ObjectAPI Primitive Types

bool, int, int32, int64, float, float32, float64, string

## Quick Example

Module file (helloworld.module.yaml):
```yaml
schema: apigear.module/1.0
name: io.world
version: "1.0.0"
interfaces:
  - name: Hello
    properties:
      - { name: last, type: Message }
    operations:
      - name: say
        params:
          - { name: msg, type: Message }
        return:
          type: int
    signals:
      - name: justSaid
        params:
          - { name: msg, type: Message }
enums:
  - name: When
    members:
      - { name: Now, value: 0 }
      - { name: Soon, value: 1 }
structs:
  - name: Message
    fields:
      - { name: content, type: string }
```

Solution file (helloworld.solution.yaml):
```yaml
schema: "apigear.solution/1.0"
name: hello_world
version: "0.1.0"
targets:
  - name: cpp_sdk
    inputs:
      - helloworld.module.yaml
    output: ../generated/cpp
    template: apigear-io/template-cpp17
    force: true
    features:
      - stubs
```

Generate: `apigear generate solution apigear/helloworld.solution.yaml`
